problem_statement: Independent nutritionists spend 4-6 hours manually creating individual meal plans, struggle to find and retain patients through fragmented digital channels, and lack integrated tools for AI-assisted diet planning and automated patient follow-up — forcing them to cobble together multiple apps (EHR, telehealth, meal planner, WhatsApp) or rely on spreadsheets.
target_user: Independent nutritionists and dietitians in LATAM seeking to manage patients digitally, and health-conscious patients looking for accessible, affordable nutritional guidance online.

current_solutions.solution: WhatsApp for patient communication and diet sharing

evidence_summary: 18 unique complaint threads found across Reddit r/dietetics, Trustpilot (Nourish 8700+ reviews), BBB (Fay), Capterra (Doctoralia, NutriAdmin), and practitioner blogs. Pattern: nutritionists spend excessive time on manual meal planning (4-6 hrs per plan), are frustrated by slow/clunky software interfaces, lack of integrated telehealth+meal planning+EHR in one platform, and in LATAM specifically, platforms like Doctoralia fail to deliver promised patient acquisition. Workarounds are widespread (spreadsheets, WhatsApp, multi-tool stacks). The paid alternative market is crowded (12+ tools) but heavily US/EU-focused — LATAM-specific solutions are scarce, with most nutritionists relying on Doctoralia or generic tools.
